1029	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1030	[Descent of a localised-module structure along a scalar tower]
1031	  \label{lem:gf_islocalizedmodule_restrictscalars}
1032	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GenericFreeness.isLocalizedModule_restrictScalars}
1033	  Let \(A_g \to R\) be a ring map with compatible \(A_g\)- and \(R\)-module structures on
1034	  \(M\) and \(M'\) (scalar towers \(A_g \to R \to M\) and \(A_g \to R \to M'\)), and let
1035	  \(S \subseteq A_g\) be a multiplicative subset. If an \(R\)-linear map
1036	  \(f : M \to M'\) realises \(M'\) as the localisation of \(M\) at the image submonoid
1037	  \(S \cdot 1 \subseteq R\) over \(R\), then its restriction of scalars to \(A_g\) realises
1038	  \(M'\) as the localisation of \(M\) at \(S\) over \(A_g\).
1039	\end{lemma}
1040	\begin{proof}
1041	  One verifies the universal property of a localised module for the restricted map. Each
1042	  \(s \in S\) acts invertibly on \(M'\): its action coincides, via the scalar tower, with
1043	  that of its image in \(R\), which is a unit because \(f\) is a localisation at the image
1044	  submonoid. Surjectivity onto \(M'\) by fractions \(x/s\) with \(s \in S\) and the
1045	  equality criterion likewise descend from the corresponding image-submonoid statements,
1046	  rewriting each \(R\)-scalar by an \(A_g\)-scalar through the scalar tower. Assembling
1047	  these conditions gives the claim.

1050	\begin{lemma}[Localisation identification across an associated denominator]
1051	  \label{lem:isLocalization_away_mul_of_associated}

1062	\begin{lemma}[Freeness transports across a semilinear ring isomorphism]
1063	  \label{lem:module_free_of_ringEquiv}
1064	  \lean{Module.Free.of_ringEquiv}
1065	  \mathlibok
1066	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
1067	  Let \(R, R'\) be rings, \(M\) an \(R\)-module and \(M'\) an \(R'\)-module, and let

1073	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1074	[L5 step-4 helper — descent of freeness across a tower of \(\mathrm{Away}\) localisations]
1075	  \label{lem:gf_away_tower_descent}
1076	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GenericFreeness.free_localizationAway_of_away_tower}
1077	  \uses{lem:isLocalization_away_mul_of_associated, lem:module_free_of_ringEquiv}
1078	  Let \(A\) be a domain and \(T\) an \(A\)-module. Let \(g \in A\), \(g \ne 0\), and let
1079	  \(h \in A_g := \mathrm{Away}(g)\) be non-zero. Write
1080	  \((T_g)_h\) for the iterated localisation of \(T\) — first away from \(g\), then away from
1081	  \(h\) — and \((A_g)_h := \mathrm{Away}(h)\) for the corresponding iterated localisation of the
1082	  base ring. If \((T_g)_h\) is free over \((A_g)_h\), then there exists \(f \in A\),
1083	  \(f \ne 0\), such that the single localisation \(T_f\) is free over \(A_f := \mathrm{Away}(f)\).
1084	  Concretely the witness is the single product \(f := g\,a\) for an element \(a \in A\) whose
1085	  image in \(A_g\) is associated to \(h\).
1086	\end{lemma}
1087	
1088	\begin{proof}
1089	  \uses{lem:isLocalization_away_mul_of_associated, lem:module_free_of_ringEquiv}
1090	  \emph{Clear the denominator of \(h\).} As \(h\) lies in \(A_g = \mathrm{Away}(g)\),
1091	  surjectivity of the localisation map \(A \to A_g\) onto fractions yields \(a \in A\) and a
1092	  power \(s\) of \(g\) with \(h \cdot \bar s = \bar a\) in \(A_g\), where \(\bar{\,\cdot\,}\)
1093	  denotes the image in \(A_g\). The image \(\bar s\) is a unit (a power of \(g\) is invertible
1094	  in \(A_g\)), so \(\bar a = h\,\bar s\) is associated to \(h\); in particular \(\bar a \ne 0\)
1095	  (because \(h \ne 0\)), hence \(a \ne 0\). Since \(A\) is a domain and \(g, a \ne 0\), the
1096	  product \(f := g\,a\) is non-zero.
1097	
1098	  \emph{Identify the base rings.} The associatedness of \(\bar a\) and \(h\), together with the
1099	  tower \(A \to A_g \to A_h\), identifies the iterated localisation
1100	  \((A_g)_h = \mathrm{Away}(h)\) with the single localisation of \(A\) away from the product
1101	  \(g\,a\): by \cref{lem:isLocalization_away_mul_of_associated} (with \(x = g\), \(z = a\),
1102	  \(y = h\)) the ring \(A_h\) is a localisation of \(A\) away from \(g\,a\), and uniqueness of
1103	  localisation furnishes a ring isomorphism \(\mathrm{Away}(g\,a) \xrightarrow{\sim} A_h\).
1104	
1105	  \emph{Identify the modules.} The composite localisation map
1106	  \[
1107	    \psi : T \longrightarrow (T_g)_h, \qquad
1108	    \psi := \mathrm{mk}_{\langle h\rangle} \circ \mathrm{mk}_{\langle g\rangle},
1109	  \]
1110	  first into \(T_g\) and then into \((T_g)_h\), realises \((T_g)_h\) as the localisation of
1111	  \(T\) at the powers of \(g\,a\): every \(g^n a^m\) acts invertibly on \((T_g)_h\), \(\psi\) is
1112	  surjective up to such denominators, and its kernel is the expected one. This
1113	  localisation-of-localisation identity is verified directly from the defining properties of a
1114	  localised module; no packaged lemma supplies it.
1115	
1116	  \emph{Transport freeness.} By hypothesis \((T_g)_h\) is free over \(A_h\). The ring
1117	  isomorphism \(\mathrm{Away}(g\,a) \cong A_h\) and the induced semilinear module equivalence
1118	  \(T_{g\,a} \simeq (T_g)_h\) — assembled from the localised-module identification of the
1119	  previous step and promoted to an \(\mathrm{Away}(g\,a)\)-linear equivalence — carry this
1120	  freeness back by \cref{lem:module_free_of_ringEquiv}: \(T_{g\,a}\) is free over
1121	  \(\mathrm{Away}(g\,a)\). Taking \(f := g\,a\) gives \(T_f\) free over \(A_f\), as required.
1122	\end{proof}
1123	
1124	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1125	[L5 — polynomial-ring core of generic freeness]
1126	  \label{lem:gf_polynomial_core}
1127	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GenericFreeness.exists_free_localizationAway_polynomial}
1128	  \uses{lem:gf_finite_module, lem:gf_splice_shortExact, lem:gf_torsion_base,
1129	        lem:gf_splice_shortExact_free_transport, lem:gf_generic_rank_ses,
1130	        lem:gf_torsion_reindex, lem:gf_away_tower_descent}
1131	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S4 ``Lemma on Generic Flatness'', proof, inductive step
1132	  % (read from references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L1760-L1772).
1133	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:
1134	  % "Let $m$ be the generic rank of the finite module $B_g$ over the domain
1135	  %  $A_g[b_1,\ldots, b_n]$. Then we have a short exact sequence of
1136	  %  $A_g[b_1,\ldots, b_n]$-modules of the form
1137	  %  $0\to A_g[b_1,\ldots, b_n]^{\oplus m} \to B_g \to T \to 0$ where $T$ is a finite
1138	  %  torsion module over $A_g[b_1,\ldots, b_n]$. Therefore, the dimension of the
1139	  %  support of $K\otimes_{A_g}T$ as a $K\otimes_{A_g}(B_g)$-module is strictly less
1140	  %  than $n$. Hence by induction on $n$ (applied to the data $A_g$, $B_g$, $T$),
1141	  %  there exists some $h\ne 0$ in $A$ with $T_h$ free over $A_{gh}$. Taking $f=gh$,
1142	  %  the lemma follows from the above short exact sequence."
1143	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4, ``Lemma on Generic Flatness'' (polynomial-ring
1144	  core of the induction).}
1145	  Let \(A\) be a noetherian domain and \(d \ge 0\). Let \(N\) be a finite module over
1146	  the polynomial ring \(A[X_1, \dots, X_d]\), regarded as an \(A\)-module. Then there
1147	  exists \(f \in A\), \(f \ne 0\), such that \(N_f\) is free over \(A_f\).
1148	
1149	\end{lemma}
1150	
1151	\begin{proof}
1152	  \uses{lem:gf_finite_module, lem:gf_splice_shortExact, lem:gf_torsion_base,
1153	        lem:gf_splice_shortExact_free_transport, lem:gf_generic_rank_ses,
1154	        lem:gf_torsion_reindex, lem:gf_away_tower_descent}
1155	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: see the comment above the statement (L1760-L1772).
1156	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4 (polynomial-ring core).}
1157	  We argue by strong induction on \(d\), \emph{generalising the base domain \(A\)} (along
1158	  with the module \(N\)): the inductive hypothesis is available for every \(d' < d\),
1159	  every noetherian domain in place of \(A\), and every finite module over the
1160	  corresponding polynomial ring.
1161	
1162	  \emph{Base case \(d = 0\).} The ring \(P_0 = A[X_1, \dots, X_0]\) is \(A\) itself, so
1163	  \(N\) is a finite \(A\)-module and the claim is exactly the finite-module leaf
1164	  \cref{lem:gf_finite_module}.
1165	
1166	  \emph{Inductive step \(d \ge 1\).} If \(N\) is already a torsion \(P_d\)-module over
1167	  \(A\) — equivalently \(N_K = 0\) for \(K = \mathrm{Frac}(A)\) — then the torsion base
1168	  case \cref{lem:gf_torsion_base} produces \(f \ne 0\) with \(N_f = 0\), which is free
1169	  over \(A_f\). Otherwise we proceed in five steps.
1170	  \begin{enumerate}
1171	    \item \textbf{Generic-rank SES.} Apply \cref{lem:gf_generic_rank_ses} to \(N\) over
1172	      \(P_d = A[X_1, \dots, X_d]\): there are \(m \in \mathbb{N}\) and an injective
1173	      \(P_d\)-linear map \(\varphi : P_d^{\oplus m} \to N\) with torsion cokernel
1174	      \(T := N / \operatorname{im}\varphi\), i.e.\ a short exact sequence of
1175	      \(P_d\)-modules
1176	      \[
1177	        0 \to P_d^{\oplus m} \xrightarrow{\ \varphi\ } N \to T \to 0,
1178	        \qquad T \text{ torsion}.
1179	      \]
1180	      No element of \(A\) is inverted at this step.
1181	    \item \textbf{Reindex the torsion cokernel.} Apply \cref{lem:gf_torsion_reindex} to the
1182	      finite torsion module \(T\): there are \(g \ne 0\) in \(A\) and \(m' < d\) such that
1183	      the localisation \(T_g\) is module-finite over the polynomial ring
1184	      \(A_g[X_1, \dots, X_{m'}]\) in fewer variables over the base ring \(A_g = A[g^{-1}]\).
1185	    \item \textbf{Inductive hypothesis at the new base \(A_g\).} Since \(A_g\) is again a
1186	      noetherian domain and \(m' < d\), the inductive hypothesis applies \emph{at base
1187	      \(A_g\)} to the finite \(A_g[X_1, \dots, X_{m'}]\)-module \(T_g\): there is
1188	      \(h \ne 0\) in \(A_g\) with \((T_g)_h\) free over \((A_g)_h\). This is exactly the
1189	      point at which the base ring of the induction must be allowed to change from \(A\)
1190	      to \(A_g\).
1191	    \item \textbf{Descend the witness from \(A_g\) to \(A\).} Clearing the denominator of
1192	      \(h\) over \(A_g\) produces a numerator \(a \in A\) whose image in \(A_g\) is associated
1193	      to \(h\); then the iterated localisation \((A_g)_h\) is the single localisation
1194	      \(A_{g a}\) and \((T_g)_h\) is \(T_{g a}\). Thus freeness of \((T_g)_h\) over
1195	      \((A_g)_h\) transports to freeness of \(T_{g a}\) over \(A_{g a}\) by the
1196	      iterated-\(\mathrm{Away}\)-tower descent \cref{lem:gf_away_tower_descent}. Set
1197	      \(f := g a \ne 0\) — the single product, \emph{not} a square — so that \(T_f\) is free
1198	      over \(A_f\).
1199	    \item \textbf{Splice.} The left end \(P_d^{\oplus m}\) of the sequence is a free
1200	      \(A\)-module (the polynomial ring \(P_d = A[X_1, \dots, X_d]\) is \(A\)-free, hence
1201	      so is any finite direct power; note \(P_d^{\oplus m}\) is \emph{not} module-finite
1202	      over \(A\), but freeness is all the splice requires), and a free module stays free
1203	      under the localisation \(A \to A_f\); so \(P_d^{\oplus m}\) localises to a free
1204	      \(A_f\)-module. With both ends free over \(A_f\) after inverting \(f = g a\),
1205	      \cref{lem:gf_splice_shortExact} splices the localised short exact sequence to
1206	      conclude that the middle term \(N_f\) is free over \(A_f\). Taking \(f = g a\)
1207	      completes the induction.
1208	  \end{enumerate}
1209	\end{proof}

1255	\subsection{Geometric bridges for the affine assembly}
1256	\label{sec:gf_geometric_bridges}
1257	
1258	The geometric form of generic flatness is assembled from the algebraic core by
1259	restricting to a non-empty affine open of \(S\) and working patchwise over a finite
1260	affine cover of the preimage. Two genuinely geometric steps are isolated here as
1261	named bridges: the affine-local finiteness of the section module of a finite-type
1262	quasi-coherent sheaf (G1), feeding the algebraic core its finite-module input; and
1263	the flat-locality assembly (G3), promoting per-patch freeness to flatness of the
1264	restricted sheaf over the base open.
1265	
1266	\begin{lemma}[Finiteness is local on a finite spanning family]
1267	  \label{lem:module_finite_of_localizationSpan}
1268	  \lean{Module.Finite.of_localizationSpan_finite}
1269	  \mathlibok
1270	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.LocalProperties.Submodule}).}
1271	  Let \(R\) be a commutative ring, \(N\) an \(R\)-module, and \(t \subseteq R\) a
1272	  finite family with \(\mathrm{span}\,t = \top\) (the \(t\) generate the unit
1273	  ideal). Suppose that for each \(g \in t\) the localization \(N_g\) is a finite
1274	  module over the localization \(R_g\). Then \(N\) is a finite \(R\)-module.
1275	\end{lemma}
1276	
1277	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1278	[Half 1 helper — transfer of localized-module finiteness across models]
1279	  \label{lem:finite_localizedModule_transfer}
1280	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.finite_localizedModule_of_isLocalizedModule}
1281	  Let \(R\) be a commutative ring, \(S \subseteq R\) a multiplicative submonoid,
1282	  and \(M\) an \(R\)-module. Let \(R_S\) be an \(R\)-algebra that localizes \(R\)
1283	  at \(S\), and let \(N\) be an \(R\)-module carrying a compatible \(R_S\)-module
1284	  structure, together with an \(R\)-linear map \(\varphi : M \to N\) exhibiting
1285	  \(N\) as the localization of \(M\) at \(S\) (\(\mathrm{IsLocalizedModule}\,S\,
1286	  \varphi\)). If \(N\) is a finite \(R_S\)-module, then the canonical localized
1287	  module \(\mathrm{LocalizedModule}\,S\,M\) is a finite module over the canonical
1288	  localization \(\mathrm{Localization}\,S\).
1289	\end{lemma}
1290	
1291	\begin{proof}
1292	  The localization map \(\varphi\) provides an \(R\)-linear isomorphism
1293	  \(e : N \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathrm{LocalizedModule}\,S\,M\) (universal property
1294	  of localized modules), and the algebra localization provides an \(R\)-algebra
1295	  isomorphism \(\psi : R_S \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathrm{Localization}\,S\). These
1296	  are compatible: \(e\) is semilinear over \(\psi\), i.e.\ \(e(a \cdot x) =
1297	  \psi(a) \cdot e(x)\) for \(a \in R_S\), \(x \in N\) (both sides are pinned by
1298	  clearing a common denominator \(s \in S\), where the \(R\)-actions agree). A
1299	  finite \(R_S\)-spanning set \(T\) of \(N\) therefore maps under \(e\) to a
1300	  finite set whose \(\mathrm{Localization}\,S\)-span is all of
1301	  \(\mathrm{LocalizedModule}\,S\,M\); hence the canonical model is finite over
1302	  \(\mathrm{Localization}\,S\).
1303	\end{proof}

1349	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1350	[Half 2, seam 1a — local generators restrict along a basic-open immersion]
1351	  \label{lem:gf_localGenerators_restrict}
1352	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gf_localGenerators_restrict}
1353	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and \(\F\) a quasi-coherent sheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules.
1354	  Suppose that on an open \(Y \subseteq X\) the restriction \(\F|_Y\) is
1355	  globally generated by finitely many sections. Then for any open immersion
1356	  \(D(g) \hookrightarrow Y\) the restricted family — the images of those generators under
1357	  the section-restriction \(\Gamma(\F, Y) \to \Gamma(\F, D(g))\) — is again a finite
1358	  generating family of \(\F|_{D(g)}\). Generation survives passage to the smaller open
1359	  (a family that generates every stalk over \(Y\) generates every stalk over \(D(g)\)),
1360	  and the index set, and hence finiteness, is unchanged.
1361	\end{lemma}
1362	
1363	\begin{proof}
1364	  A family of sections \(\{s_i\}_{i \in I}\) generates \(\F|_Y\) precisely when the
1365	  induced map \(\OO_Y^{\oplus I} \to \F|_Y\) is surjective on every stalk
1366	  \(\F_y\,(y \in Y)\). Restricting along the open immersion \(D(g) \hookrightarrow Y\) the
1367	  generators map to \(\{s_i|_{D(g)}\}_{i \in I}\), and for a point \(x \in D(g)\) the stalk
1368	  \(\F_x\) and the comparison map agree with the value at \(x\) computed over \(Y\), since
1369	  stalks are insensitive to shrinking the ambient open. Hence the induced map
1370	  \(\OO_{D(g)}^{\oplus I} \to \F|_{D(g)}\) is surjective on every stalk over \(D(g)\), so
1371	  \(\{s_i|_{D(g)}\}_{i \in I}\) generates \(\F|_{D(g)}\). The index set \(I\) is the same
1372	  finite set, so finiteness is preserved.
1373	\end{proof}
1374	
1375	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1376	[Half 2, seam 1b — finite standard-basic-open subcover of an affine open]
1377	  \label{lem:gf_affine_finite_standard_subcover}
1378	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gf_affine_finite_standard_subcover}
1379	  Let \(W\) be an affine open of a scheme \(X\), with the canonical identification
1380	  \(W \cong \Spec \Gamma(X, W)\). Given an arbitrary open cover \(\{U_\alpha\}\) of \(W\),
1381	  there is a finite family \(t \subseteq \Gamma(X, W)\) with
1382	  \(\mathrm{Ideal.span}\,t = \top\) such that each basic open \(D(g)\) (\(g \in t\)) is
1383	  contained in some cover member \(U_\alpha\). In particular the \(D(g)\,(g \in t)\) form a
1384	  finite standard-basic-open subcover of \(W\) refining \(\{U_\alpha\}\).
1385	\end{lemma}
1386	
1387	\begin{proof}
1388	  Under \(W \cong \Spec \Gamma(X, W)\) the basic opens \(D(g)\,(g \in \Gamma(X, W))\) form a
1389	  basis of the topology (\(\mathrm{PrimeSpectrum.isBasis\_basic\_opens}\)). Hence each
1390	  cover member \(U_\alpha\) is a union of basic opens, and the collection of all basic
1391	  opens \(D(g)\) that are contained in some \(U_\alpha\) still covers \(W\). The affine
1392	  scheme \(W\) is quasi-compact, so finitely many of these basic opens
1393	  \(D(g_1), \dots, D(g_n)\) already cover \(W\); set
1394	  \(t = \{g_1, \dots, g_n\} \subseteq \Gamma(X, W)\), a finite family, each \(D(g_i)\)
1395	  contained in some \(U_\alpha\) by construction. That the \(D(g_i)\) cover
1396	  \(W = \Spec \Gamma(X, W)\) — i.e. no prime ideal contains all the \(g_i\) — is exactly
1397	  the condition \(\mathrm{Ideal.span}\,t = \top\).
1398	\end{proof}

1400	\begin{lemma}[Half 2, seam 1c — finite generation equals a finite free epimorphism]
1401	  \label{lem:gf_finite_gen_iff_free_epi}
1402	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gf_finite_gen_iff_free_epi}
1403	  Let \(Y\) be an open of a scheme and \(\F\) a sheaf of modules on \(Y\). Then
1404	  \(\F\) is globally generated by a finite family of sections if and only if there is a
1405	  finite index set \(I\) and an epimorphism of \(\OO_Y\)-modules
1406	  \(\OO_Y^{\oplus I} \twoheadrightarrow \F\). No quasi-coherence of \(\F\) is assumed:
1407	  the statement holds for arbitrary sheaves of modules on \(Y\), and applies in particular
1408	  to the restrictions \(\F|_{D(g)}\) arising in the cover assembly.
1409	\end{lemma}
1410	
1411	\begin{proof}
1412	  A finite family of sections \(\{s_i\}_{i \in I}\) of \(\F|_Y\) is the same datum as an
1413	  \(\OO_Y\)-linear map \(\OO_Y^{\oplus I} \to \F|_Y\) sending the \(i\)-th basis section to
1414	  \(s_i\). The family generates \(\F|_Y\) (the induced map is surjective on every stalk)
1415	  exactly when this map is an epimorphism of sheaves of modules. Thus a finite generating
1416	  family yields a finite-free epimorphism, and conversely the images of the basis sections
1417	  of any epimorphism \(\OO_Y^{\oplus I} \twoheadrightarrow \F|_Y\) with \(I\) finite form a
1418	  finite generating family.
1419	\end{proof}
1420	
1421	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1422	[Half 2, seam 1 — finite type yields a finite cover of globally-generated affines]
1423	  \label{lem:gf_finiteType_affine_finite_cover_generated}
1424	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gf_finiteType_affine_finite_cover_generated}
1425	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], "Properties of Schemes", Tag 01PB, proof
1426	  % (read from references/stacks-properties.tex, L2101-L2107).
1427	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:
1428	  % "Assume $\widetilde M$ is a finite type $\mathcal{O}_X$-module. This means
1429	  %  there exists an open covering of $X$ such that $\widetilde M$ restricted to
1430	  %  the members of this covering is globally generated by finitely many sections.
1431	  %  Thus there also exists a standard open covering
1432	  %  $X = \bigcup_{i = 1, \ldots, n} D(f_i)$ such that $\widetilde M|_{D(f_i)}$
1433	  %  is generated by finitely many sections."
1434	  \uses{lem:gf_localGenerators_restrict, lem:gf_affine_finite_standard_subcover,
1435	        lem:gf_finite_gen_iff_free_epi}
1436	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], "Properties of Schemes", Tag 01PB (standard
1437	  cover of generated affines).}
1438	  Let \(X\) be a scheme, \(\F\) a quasi-coherent sheaf of modules of finite type,
1439	  and \(W \subseteq X\) an affine open. Then there is a finite family
1440	  \(t \subseteq \Gamma(X, W)\) with \(\mathrm{Ideal.span}\,t = \top\) such that for
1441	  each \(g \in t\) the basic open \(D(g)\) is affine and \(\F|_{D(g)}\) is globally
1442	  generated by finitely many sections; equivalently, for each \(g \in t\) there is
1443	  a finite index set \(I_g\) and an epimorphism of \(\OO_{D(g)}\)-modules
1444	  \(\OO_{D(g)}^{\oplus I_g} \twoheadrightarrow \F|_{D(g)}\).
1445	\end{lemma}
1446	
1447	\begin{proof}
1448	  \uses{lem:gf_localGenerators_restrict, lem:gf_affine_finite_standard_subcover,
1449	        lem:gf_finite_gen_iff_free_epi}
1450	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], Tag 01PB (standard cover of generated affines).}
1451	  Finite type of \(\F\) means that \(X\) has an open covering \(\{Y_\alpha\}\) on each
1452	  member of which \(\F\) carries a finite generating family of sections. The intersections
1453	  \(\{Y_\alpha \cap W\}\) form an open cover of the affine \(W\). By the finite standard
1454	  subcover refinement \cref{lem:gf_affine_finite_standard_subcover} there is a finite family
1455	  \(t = \{g_1, \dots, g_n\} \subseteq \Gamma(X, W)\) with
1456	  \(\mathrm{Ideal.span}\,t = \top\) and each basic open \(D(g_i)\) contained in some
1457	  \(Y_\alpha \cap W\) — in particular \(D(g_i) \subseteq W\) is affine, and \(D(g_i)\)
1458	  embeds by an open immersion into the member \(Y_\alpha\) on which \(\F\) is finitely
1459	  generated. By the restriction-of-generators lemma
1460	  \cref{lem:gf_localGenerators_restrict} the finite generating family on \(Y_\alpha\)
1461	  restricts to a finite generating family of \(\F|_{D(g_i)}\), so \(\F|_{D(g_i)}\) is
1462	  globally generated by finitely many sections. Finally, by the free-epimorphism
1463	  reformulation \cref{lem:gf_finite_gen_iff_free_epi}, this is equivalent to the existence
1464	  of a finite index set \(I_{g_i}\) and an epimorphism of \(\OO_{D(g_i)}\)-modules
1465	  \(\OO_{D(g_i)}^{\oplus I_{g_i}} \twoheadrightarrow \F|_{D(g_i)}\). That the \(D(g_i)\)
1466	  cover \(W = \Spec \Gamma(X, W)\) is exactly \(\mathrm{Ideal.span}\,t = \top\). This is
1467	  Stacks' passage from the local-generation datum to a standard finite cover of generated
1468	  affines.
1469	\end{proof}

1471	\begin{lemma}[Affine $\widetilde{(-)}\dashv\Gamma$ adjunction with its $\mathrm{fromTildeΓ}$ counit]
1472	  \label{lem:tilde_adjunction_mathlib}
1473	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.adjunction}
1474	  \mathlibok
1475	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib (\texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Tilde}).}
1476	  On the affine scheme \(\Spec R\) the associated-sheaf functor
1477	  \(\widetilde{(-)} : \mathrm{Mod}\,R \to (\Spec R).\mathrm{Modules}\) is left adjoint to the
1478	  affine section functor \(\Gamma : (\Spec R).\mathrm{Modules} \to \mathrm{Mod}\,R\) (Mathlib's
1479	  \(\mathrm{moduleSpecΓFunctor}\), evaluation of a sheaf of modules at the top open). The counit of
1480	  this adjunction is the natural transformation
1481	  \(\Gamma(-)\,\widetilde{\phantom{x}} \to \mathrm{id}\) whose component at a sheaf \(\mathcal{M}\)
1482	  is \(\mathcal{M}.\mathrm{fromTildeΓ} : \widetilde{\Gamma(\mathcal{M}, \top)} \to \mathcal{M}\), and
1483	  the left adjoint \(\widetilde{(-)}\) is faithful.
1484	\end{lemma}

1486	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1487	[Half 2, seam 2 — $\Gamma$ on affine quasi-coherent modules sends epis to surjections]
1488	  \label{lem:gf_affine_qcoh_Gamma_epi}
1489	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gf_affine_qcoh_Gamma_epi}
1490	  \uses{lem:qcoh_affine_section_localization, lem:tilde_adjunction_mathlib,
1491	    lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_isLocalizedModule_restrict}
1492	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], "Properties of Schemes", Tag 01PB, proof
1493	  % (read from references/stacks-properties.tex, L2106-L2107) — the implication
1494	  % "$\widetilde M|_{D(f_i)}$ generated by finitely many sections $\Rightarrow$
1495	  % $M_{f_i}$ finitely generated" is exactly section-level surjectivity of a sheaf
1496	  % epi over an affine.
1497	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:
1498	  % "such that $\widetilde M|_{D(f_i)}$ is generated by finitely many sections.
1499	  %  Thus $M_{f_i}$ is finitely generated for each $i$."
1500	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], "Properties of Schemes", Tag 01PB (sections of a
1501	  generated sheaf over an affine).}
1502	  Let \(V\) be an affine open of a scheme, with \(B = \Gamma(X, V)\) and
1503	  \(V \cong \Spec B\), and let \(\pi : \mathcal{G} \to \F\) be an epimorphism of
1504	  quasi-coherent sheaves of modules over \(V\). Then the induced map on sections
1505	  \(\Gamma(\pi) : \Gamma(\mathcal{G}, V) \to \Gamma(\F, V)\) is a surjective
1506	  \(B\)-module map.
1507	\end{lemma}
1508	
1509	\begin{proof}
1510	  \uses{lem:qcoh_affine_section_localization, lem:tilde_adjunction_mathlib,
1511	    lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_isLocalizedModule_restrict}
1512	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], Tag 01PB (sections of a generated sheaf over an
1513	  affine).}
1514	  Write \(V \cong \Spec B\) and let \(\Gamma\) be the affine section functor on
1515	  \((\Spec B).\mathrm{Modules}\), so that \(\Gamma(\pi) = \Gamma(\mathcal{G}, V) \to
1516	  \Gamma(\F, V)\) is the map in question. By \cref{lem:tilde_adjunction_mathlib} the
1517	  associated-sheaf functor \(\widetilde{(-)}\) is left adjoint to \(\Gamma\), with counit the
1518	  natural transformation whose component at a sheaf \(\mathcal{M}\) is
1519	  \(\mathcal{M}.\mathrm{fromTildeΓ} : \widetilde{\Gamma(\mathcal{M}, V)} \to \mathcal{M}\).
1520	
1521	  Because \(\mathcal{G}\) and \(\F\) are quasi-coherent, the qcoh-sections descent
1522	  (\cref{lem:qcoh_affine_section_localization}, fed through the counit-iso assembly
1523	  \cref{lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_isLocalizedModule_restrict}) makes both counit components
1524	  \(\mathcal{G}.\mathrm{fromTildeΓ}\) and \(\F.\mathrm{fromTildeΓ}\) isomorphisms. Naturality of the
1525	  counit on \(\pi\) gives the commuting square
1526	  \[
1527	    \widetilde{\Gamma(\pi)} \circ \mathcal{G}.\mathrm{fromTildeΓ}^{-1}
1528	      = \F.\mathrm{fromTildeΓ}^{-1} \circ \pi,
1529	    \qquad\text{equivalently}\qquad
1530	    \widetilde{\Gamma(\pi)}
1531	      = \F.\mathrm{fromTildeΓ}^{-1} \circ \pi \circ \mathcal{G}.\mathrm{fromTildeΓ}.
1532	  \]
1533	  The right-hand side is a composite of an isomorphism, the epimorphism \(\pi\), and an
1534	  isomorphism, hence is itself an epimorphism. Thus \(\widetilde{\Gamma(\pi)}\) is an epimorphism
1535	  of sheaves of modules. Since the left adjoint \(\widetilde{(-)}\) is faithful, it reflects
1536	  epimorphisms; therefore \(\Gamma(\pi)\) is an epimorphism in \(\mathrm{Mod}\,B\), i.e. the
1537	  \(B\)-linear map \(\Gamma(\pi)\) is surjective.
1538	
1539	  This is the structural content of the vanishing of higher quasi-coherent cohomology on an
1540	  affine — the failure of \(\Gamma\) to be right exact would obstruct exactly this surjectivity —
1541	  obtained here directly from the affine adjunction without invoking a cohomology computation.
1542	\end{proof}
1543

1558	  quasi-coherent sheaves of modules over \(V\). Suppose the source has finite sections:

1655	\begin{lemma}[G3 — flat-locality assembly from per-patch freeness]
1656	  \label{lem:gf_flat_locality_assembly}
1657	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gf_flat_locality_assembly}
1658	  \uses{lem:gf_qcoh_fintype_finite_sections, lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen}
1659	  Let \(p : X \to S\) be a finite-type morphism with \(S\) integral and locally
1660	  noetherian, and \(\F\) a finite-type quasi-coherent \(\OO_X\)-module. Fix a non-empty
1661	  affine open \(U_0 = \Spec A \subseteq S\) with \(A\) a noetherian domain, a finite
1662	  affine cover \(\{W_j = \Spec B_j\}_j\) of \(p^{-1}(U_0)\), and an element
1663	  \(f \in A\), \(f \ne 0\), such that for every \(j\) the localized section module
1664	  \((M_j)_f\) (with \(M_j = \Gamma(\F, W_j)\)) is free over \(A_f\). Set
1665	  \(V = D(f) \subseteq U_0\). Then for every affine open \(U \le V\) of \(S\) and every
1666	  affine open \(W \le p^{-1}(U)\) of \(X\), the section module \(\Gamma(\F, W)\) is flat
1667	  over \(\Gamma(S, U)\).
1668	\end{lemma}
1669	
1670	\begin{proof}
1671	  \uses{lem:gf_qcoh_fintype_finite_sections, lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen}
1672	  Throughout, flatness is established by the standard locality criteria for module flatness
1673	  over a commutative ring, applied on the \emph{base} ring; the source cover \(\{W_j\}\)
1674	  enters only through the stalks of \(\F\), and is never treated as a covering family of any
1675	  base ring.
1676	
1677	  \emph{Step 1 — free \(\Rightarrow\) flat on each patch.} Each \((M_j)_f\) is free over
1678	  \(A_f\) by hypothesis, and a free module is flat. Hence each \((M_j)_f\) is flat over
1679	  \(A_f\).
1680	
1681	  \emph{Step 2 — pointwise flatness of \(\F\) over the base on \(p^{-1}(V)\).} Fix a point
1682	  \(x \in p^{-1}(V) = X_V\) and put \(s = p(x) \in V = D(f)\). The cover \(\{W_j\}\) of
1683	  \(p^{-1}(U_0) \supseteq p^{-1}(V)\) contains a patch \(W_j \ni x\). On \(W_j\),
1684	  quasi-coherence identifies \(\F|_{W_j}\) with the associated sheaf \(\widetilde{M_j}\) of
1685	  the finite \(B_j\)-module \(M_j = \Gamma(\F, W_j)\) (finite by
1686	  \cref{lem:gf_qcoh_fintype_finite_sections}); consequently the sections of \(\F\) over a
1687	  basic open \(D(g) \subseteq W_j\) are the localization \((M_j)_g\)
1688	  (\cref{lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen}), and passing to the limit the stalk
1689	  \(\F_x\) is the localization of \(M_j\) at the prime of \(B_j\) corresponding to \(x\).
1690	  Because \(s \in D(f)\), the element \(f\) is invertible in the local ring \(\OO_{S,s}\) and
1691	  hence acts invertibly on \(\F_x\); thus \(\F_x\) is equally a localization of the inverted
1692	  module \((M_j)_f\). The local ring \(\OO_{S,s}\) is the localization of \(A_f\) at the prime
1693	  of \(s\) (a point of \(D(f) = \Spec A_f\)), and localization preserves flatness; since
1694	  \((M_j)_f\) is flat over \(A_f\), the localized module \(\F_x\) is flat over \(\OO_{S,s}\).
1695	  Therefore \(\F\) is flat over the base at every point of \(p^{-1}(V)\): the stalk \(\F_x\)
1696	  is a flat \(\OO_{S,p(x)}\)-module for all \(x \in p^{-1}(V)\).
1697	
1698	  \emph{Step 3 — from pointwise flatness to \(\Gamma(\F, W)\) flat over \(\Gamma(S, U)\).}
1699	  Let \(U \le V\) and \(W \le p^{-1}(U)\) be affine, and write \(R = \Gamma(S, U)\),
1700	  \(B = \Gamma(X, W)\), and \(N = \Gamma(\F, W)\); here \(N\) is a \(B\)-module and \(B\) an
1701	  \(R\)-algebra. We prove \(N\) flat over \(R\) by reducing first on the base, then on the
1702	  source.
1703	
1704	  \emph{Base reduction.} By the base-maximal locality criterion for flatness it suffices to
1705	  show, for every maximal ideal \(\mathfrak p \subset R\), that the localization
1706	  \(N_{\mathfrak p}\) is flat over \(R\); equivalently, that \(N_{\mathfrak p}\) is flat over
1707	  the local ring \(R_{\mathfrak p} = \OO_{S,x}\), where \(x \in U \subseteq V = D(f)\) is the
1708	  point corresponding to \(\mathfrak p\). In particular \(\OO_{S,x}\) is a localization of
1709	  \(A_f\).
1710	
1711	  \emph{Source reduction.} Flatness of \(N_{\mathfrak p}\) over the local base
1712	  \(R_{\mathfrak p} = \OO_{S,x}\) is checked at the points of the source: it suffices that
1713	  for every point \(y \in W\) lying over a generization of \(x\) the stalk \(\F_y\) — the
1714	  localization of \(N_{\mathfrak p}\) at the prime of \(B \otimes_R R_{\mathfrak p}\)
1715	  corresponding to \(y\) — is flat over \(R_{\mathfrak p} = \OO_{S,x}\). Each such \(y\) lies
1716	  in \(p^{-1}(V)\), so by Step 2 \(\F_y\) is flat over \(\OO_{S,p(y)}\). As \(p(y)\) is a
1717	  generization of \(x\), the comparison \(\OO_{S,x} \to \OO_{S,p(y)}\) is a localization,
1718	  hence flat, and transitivity of flatness upgrades ``\(\F_y\) flat over \(\OO_{S,p(y)}\)''
1719	  to ``\(\F_y\) flat over \(\OO_{S,x}\)''. The passage from these stalk-flatness statements
1720	  to flatness of \(N_{\mathfrak p}\) over \(R_{\mathfrak p}\) records that module flatness
1721	  over a local base ring is detected at the points of the source affine.
1722	
1723	  Combining the two reductions, \(N_{\mathfrak p}\) is flat over \(R_{\mathfrak p}\) for
1724	  every maximal ideal \(\mathfrak p \subset R\); hence \(N = \Gamma(\F, W)\) is flat over
1725	  \(R = \Gamma(S, U)\), as claimed.
1726	\end{proof}

1747	  Let \(S\) be a noetherian integral scheme, \(p : X \to S\) a finite-type morphism,
1748	  and \(\F\) a coherent \(\OO_X\)-module. Then there exists a non-empty open
1749	  subscheme \(U \subseteq S\) such that the restriction of \(\F\) to
1750	  \(X_U = p^{-1}(U)\) is \(\OO_U\)-flat.

1753	  need not become flat over any non-empty open of \(S\).

1756	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "The above theorem has the following consequence, which

1758	% (Source: nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L1776-L1777. The
1759	% Nitsure proof is the single sentence quoted: deduce the geometric form from
1760	% the algebraic generic flatness statement (\cref{thm:generic_flatness_algebraic})
1761	% by restricting to a non-empty
1762	% affine open of \(S\), where the situation reduces to the algebraic statement.)
1763	\begin{proof}
1764	  \uses{thm:generic_flatness_algebraic, lem:gf_qcoh_fintype_finite_sections,
1765	        lem:gf_flat_locality_assembly}
1766	  Restrict to a non-empty affine open \(U_0 = \Spec A \subseteq S\), where \(A\) is a
1767	  noetherian domain (\(S\) integral and locally noetherian). We assemble the witness
1768	  \(V\) from the algebraic form on a finite affine cover.
1769	
1770	  \emph{Step 1 — finite affine cover above \(U_0\).} Since \(p\) is quasi-compact (by
1771	  the \([\mathrm{QuasiCompact}\,p]\) hypothesis) and \(U_0\) is affine hence
1772	  quasi-compact, the preimage \(X_{U_0} = p^{-1}(U_0)\) is quasi-compact; choose a
1773	  finite affine cover \(\{W_j = \Spec B_j\}_{j}\) of \(X_{U_0}\) by affine opens of
1774	  \(X\). Because \(p\) is locally of finite type, each ring \(B_j = \Gamma(X, W_j)\)
1775	  is a finite-type \(A\)-algebra.
1776	
1777	  \emph{Step 2 — read off a finite module on each patch.} On each \(W_j\), quasi-coherence
1778	  of \(\F\) identifies \(\F|_{W_j}\) with the associated sheaf \(\widetilde{M_j}\) of the
1779	  section module \(M_j = \Gamma(\F, W_j)\), and the finite-section bridge
1780	  \cref{lem:gf_qcoh_fintype_finite_sections} — applied to the affine open \(W_j\) — makes
1781	  \(M_j\) a finite \(B_j\)-module. Through the scalar tower \(A \to B_j \to M_j\),
1782	  \(M_j\) is the data fed to the algebraic form.
1783	
1784	  \emph{Step 3 — apply the algebraic form and take the common basic open.}
1785	  \cref{thm:generic_flatness_algebraic} applied to \((A, B_j, M_j)\) yields \(f_j \in A\),
1786	  \(f_j \ne 0\), with \((M_j)_{f_j}\) free over \(A_{f_j}\). As the cover is finite, set
1787	  \(f := \prod_j f_j \ne 0\) (a non-zero element of the domain \(A\)) and
1788	  \(V := D(f) \subseteq \Spec A \subseteq S\), a non-empty basic open; over \(V\) every
1789	  \((M_j)_f\) is free over \(A_f\).
1790	
1791	  \emph{Step 4 — freeness implies flatness.} On each patch the localized section module
1792	  is free, hence flat over \(A_f\); the flat-locality assembly
1793	  \cref{lem:gf_flat_locality_assembly} promotes these per-patch freeness statements to
1794	  the fibrewise flatness conclusion, via the criterion that a module flat at every
1795	  maximal ideal is flat together with the locality of flatness on the source cover. This
1796	  produces the required \(V\) with \(\F|_{X_V}\) flat over \(\OO_V\).
1797	\end{proof}

2406	
2407	`GeneratingSections.map` is the `GeneratingSections` analogue of Mathlib's

2419	
2420	universe v₁ u₁ v₂ u₂
2421	
2422	variable {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v₁} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C} {R : Sheaf J RingCat.{u}}
2423	  [HasWeakSheafify J AddCommGrpCat.{u}] [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat.{u}]
2424	  [J.HasSheafCompose (forget₂ RingCat.{u} AddCommGrpCat.{u})]
2425	  {C' : Type u₂} [Category.{v₂} C'] {J' : GrothendieckTopology C'} {S : Sheaf J' RingCat.{u}}
2426	  [HasSheafify J' AddCommGrpCat.{u}] [J'.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat.{u}]
2427	  [J'.HasSheafCompose (forget₂ RingCat.{u} AddCommGrpCat.{u})]

2429	/-- Transport a generating family of `M` along a colimit-preserving functor `F` carrying
2430	the unit to the unit (`η : F.obj (unit R) ≅ unit S`): the images
2431	`(mapFree F η σ.I).inv ≫ F.map σ.π` of the generators generate `F.obj M`, with the index
2432	type unchanged.

2434	The colimit-preservation hypothesis `hF` is taken as an *explicit* argument (rather than an

2436	`PreservesColimitsOfSize` on the `def`-backed `Scheme.Modules` functors (e.g.
2437	`Scheme.Modules.pullback`) does not reliably fire through the `Modules` type abbreviation. -/
2438	noncomputable def map {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R} (σ : M.GeneratingSections)
2439	    (F : SheafOfModules.{u} R ⥤ SheafOfModules.{u} S)
2440	    (η : F.obj (SheafOfModules.unit R) ≅ SheafOfModules.unit S)
2441	    (hF : PreservesColimitsOfSize.{u, u} F) :
2442	    (F.obj M).GeneratingSections where
2443	  I := σ.I
2444	  s := (F.obj M).freeHomEquiv ((letI := hF; SheafOfModules.mapFree F η σ.I).inv ≫ F.map σ.π)
2445	  epi := by
2446	    letI := hF
2447	    letI : PreservesColimitsOfSize.{0, 0} F := preservesColimitsOfSize_shrink _
2448	    rw [Equiv.symm_apply_apply]
2449	    haveI : Epi (F.map σ.π) := inferInstance
2450	    exact epi_comp _ _

2454	    (F : SheafOfModules.{u} R ⥤ SheafOfModules.{u} S)
2455	    (η : F.obj (SheafOfModules.unit R) ≅ SheafOfModules.unit S)
2456	    (hF : PreservesColimitsOfSize.{u, u} F) :
2457	    (map σ F η hF).I = σ.I := rfl

2464	    (η : F.obj (SheafOfModules.unit R) ≅ SheafOfModules.unit S)
2465	    (hF : PreservesColimitsOfSize.{u, u} F) :
2466	    (map σ F η hF).IsFiniteType :=
2467	  ⟨inferInstanceAs (Finite σ.I)⟩

2549	Over a noetherian integral base `S`, a coherent sheaf on a finite-type
2550	`X ⟶ S` is flat above some non-empty open `V ⊆ S`. This is the inductive

2553	finite stratification of `S` by flatness loci.

2557	`B`-module `M`, there exists a non-zero `f ∈ A` such that `M_f` is a
2558	free `A_f`-module. The geometric form (this declaration) restricts to a
2559	non-empty affine open `Spec A ⊆ S` and applies the algebraic form on

2561	
2562	Blueprint reference: `thm:generic_flatness` (Nitsure §4). -/

2568	`V ⊆ S` such that `𝓕|_{X_V} = 𝓕|_{p⁻¹V}` is flat over `𝓞_V`.

The blueprint sketch is not wrong but does not describe the `GeneratingSections.map` transport engine used, which is entirely invisible to it.
